steps.description: Library strategy: eCLIP-seq
steps.code_example:
# --- Installation (commented out) ---
# Clone the eCLIP-seq-pipeline repository
# git clone https://github.com/YeoLab/eCLIP-seq-pipeline.git
# cd eCLIP-seq-pipeline

# Create and activate the conda environment using the provided environment.yml
# conda env create -f environment.yml
# conda activate eclip_pipeline_env

# --- Example config.yaml (commented out) ---
# Create a file named 'config.yaml' in your project directory.
# This is a simplified example; refer to the pipeline's documentation for a complete list of parameters.
# # config.yaml
# project_name: my_eclip_project
# genome_assembly: hg38 # or mm10, etc.
# threads: 8
#
# # Define samples and their corresponding raw FASTQ files and input controls
# samples:
#   sample1_rep1:
#     r1: /path/to/raw_data/sample1_rep1_R1.fastq.gz
#     r2: /path/to/raw_data/sample1_rep1_R2.fastq.gz # Optional, if paired-end
#     input_r1: /path/to/raw_data/sample1_input_R1.fastq.gz
#     input_r2: /path/to/raw_data/sample1_input_R2.fastq.gz # Optional, if paired-end
#   sample2_rep1:
#     r1: /path/to/raw_data/sample2_rep1_R1.fastq.gz
#     input_r1: /path/to/raw_data/sample2_input_R1.fastq.gz
#
# # Paths to pre-indexed reference files (STAR index, genome FASTA, blacklist BED, etc.)
# # These paths must be correctly set up for your chosen genome_assembly.
# # star_index: /path/to/your/star_index/hg38
# # genome_fasta: /path/to/your/genome_fasta/hg38.fa
# # blacklist_bed: /path/to/your/blacklist/hg38.bed
# # ... (other reference paths like gtf, repeatmasker, etc.)

# --- Execution ---
# Ensure you are in the directory where 'run_eclip_pipeline.py' is located,
# or provide the full path to the script.
# Also ensure your 'config.yaml' is accessible from the execution location.
# Replace 'config.yaml' with the actual path to your configuration file.
# Replace '/path/to/results' with your desired output directory.
python run_eclip_pipeline.py --config config.yaml --output_dir /path/to/results

steps.description: Takes output from raw files.
steps.code_example:
# Installation instructions (commented out)
# conda install -c bioconda fastp

# Execution command: Processes raw paired-end FASTQ files for quality control and trimming.
# -i: Input R1 FASTQ file
# -I: Input R2 FASTQ file
# -o: Output R1 cleaned FASTQ file
# -O: Output R2 cleaned FASTQ file
# --json: Output JSON report for quality metrics
# --html: Output HTML report for quality metrics
# --detect_adapter_for_pe: Automatically detect and remove adapters for paired-end reads
# --qualified_quality_phred 15: Trim bases with quality score < 15
# --length_required 30: Discard reads shorter than 30 bp after trimming
fastp -i input_R1.fastq.gz -I input_R2.fastq.gz -o output_R1.fastq.gz -O output_R2.fastq.gz \
    --json fastp.json --html fastp.html \
    --detect_adapter_for_pe \
    --qualified_quality_phred 15 \
    --length_required 30

steps.description: Run to trim off both 5â and 3â adapters on both reads.
steps.code_example:
# Installation (uncomment to install via conda)
# conda install -c bioconda fastp

# Execution: Trim adapters from paired-end reads and generate reports
# Replace 'read1.fastq.gz', 'read2.fastq.gz' with your actual input files
# Replace 'read1_trimmed.fastq.gz', 'read2_trimmed.fastq.gz' with your desired output file names
# 'fastp.json' and 'fastp.html' will contain trimming statistics
fastp -i read1.fastq.gz -I read2.fastq.gz \
      -o read1_trimmed.fastq.gz -O read2_trimmed.fastq.gz \
      --json fastp.json --html fastp.html

steps.description: Command: quality-cutoff 6 -m 18 -a NNNNNAGATCGGAAGAGCACACGTCTGAACTCCAGTCAC -g CTTCCGATCTACAAGTT -g CTTCCGATCTTGGTCCT -A AACTTGTAGATCGGA -A AGGACCAAGATCGGA -A ACTTGTAGATCGGAA -A GGACCAAGATCGGAA -A CTTGT AGATCGGAAG -A GACCAAGATCGGAAG -A TTGTAGATCGGAAGA -A ACCAAGATCGGAAGA -A TGTAGATCGGAAGAG -A CCAAGATCGGAAGAG -A GTAGATCGGAAGAGC -A CAAGATCGGAAGAGC -A TAGATCGGAAGAGCG -A AAGATCGGAAGAGCG -A AGATCGGAAGAGCGT -A GATCGGAAGAGCGTC -A ATCGGAAGAGCGTCG -A TCGGAAGAGCGTCGT -A CGGAAGAGCGTCGTG -A GGAAGAGCGTCGTGT -o /full/path/to/files/file_R1.C01.fastq.gz.adapterTrim.fastq.gz -p /full/path/to/files/file_R2.C01.fastq.gz.adapterTrim.fastq.gz /full/path/to/files/file_R1.C01.fastq.gz /full/path/to/files/file_R2.C01.fastq.gz > /full/path/to/files/file_R1.C01.fastq.gz.adapterTrim.metrics
steps.code_example:
# Install BBMap (which includes bbduk.sh) via Bioconda
# conda install -c bioconda bbmap

# Execute the command as provided (assuming 'quality-cutoff' is an alias or wrapper for bbduk.sh)
quality-cutoff 6 -m 18 -a NNNNNAGATCGGAAGAGCACACGTCTGAACTCCAGTCAC -g CTTCCGATCTACAAGTT -g CTTCCGATCTTGGTCCT -A AACTTGTAGATCGGA -A AGGACCAAGATCGGA -A ACTTGTAGATCGGAA -A GGACCAAGATCGGAA -A CTTGT AGATCGGAAG -A GACCAAGATCGGAAG -A TTGTAGATCGGAAGA -A ACCAAGATCGGAAGA -A TGTAGATCGGAAGAG -A CCAAGATCGGAAGAG -A GTAGATCGGAAGAGC -A CAAGATCGGAAGAGC -A TAGATCGGAAGAGCG -A AAGATCGGAAGAGCG -A AGATCGGAAGAGCGT -A GATCGGAAGAGCGTC -A ATCGGAAGAGCGTCG -A TCGGAAGAGCGTCGT -A CGGAAGAGCGTCGTG -A GGAAGAGCGTCGTGT -o /full/path/to/files/file_R1.C01.fastq.gz.adapterTrim.fastq.gz -p /full/path/to/files/file_R2.C01.fastq.gz.adapterTrim.fastq.gz /full/path/to/files/file_R1.C01.fastq.gz /full/path/to/files/file_R2.C01.fastq.gz > /full/path/to/files/file_R1.C01.fastq.gz.adapterTrim.metrics

steps.description: Takes output from cutadapt round 1.
steps.code_example:
# Install cutadapt (if not already installed)
# conda install -c bioconda cutadapt

# Define input and output files
# Assuming 'round1_trimmed_R1.fastq.gz' and 'round1_trimmed_R2.fastq.gz'
# are the output files from the first cutadapt round.
INPUT_R1="round1_trimmed_R1.fastq.gz"
INPUT_R2="round1_trimmed_R2.fastq.gz"
OUTPUT_R1="round2_trimmed_R1.fastq.gz"
OUTPUT_R2="round2_trimmed_R2.fastq.gz"
REPORT="cutadapt_round2_report.txt"

# Execute cutadapt for a second round of processing.
# This step typically performs additional quality trimming, length filtering,
# or removal of secondary adapters not targeted in the first round.
# Adjust parameters (-a/-A for adapters, -q for quality, --minimum-length) as needed.
cutadapt \
  -q 20,20 \ 
  --minimum-length 50 \ 
  -o "${OUTPUT_R1}" \ 
  -p "${OUTPUT_R2}" \ 
  "${INPUT_R1}" \ 
  "${INPUT_R2}" \
  > "${REPORT}" 2>&1

steps.description: Run to trim off the 3â adapters on read 2, to control for double ligation events.
steps.code_example:
# Install cutadapt using conda
# conda install -c bioconda cutadapt=4.4

# Define input and output file paths
INPUT_R1="input_R1.fastq.gz"
INPUT_R2="input_R2.fastq.gz"
OUTPUT_R1="output_R1_trimmed.fastq.gz"
OUTPUT_R2="output_R2_trimmed.fastq.gz"

# Define the 3' adapter sequence for Read 2.
# This sequence needs to be replaced with the actual adapter sequence used in your library preparation.
# For Illumina paired-end sequencing, this is typically the reverse complement of the adapter ligated to the 3' end of the insert on Read 1 (e.g., the P7 adapter sequence).
# Example for TruSeq: AGATCGGAAGAGCGTCGTGTAGGGAAAGAGTGT
ADAPTER_SEQUENCE_R2="YOUR_3PRIME_ADAPTER_SEQUENCE_FOR_READ2"

# Minimum read length after trimming. Reads shorter than this will be discarded.
# This helps control for double ligation events where only adapter sequences are present, resulting in very short reads after adapter removal.
MIN_READ_LENGTH=20

# Run cutadapt to trim the 3' adapter from Read 2
# -A: Adapter sequence to be removed from the 3' end of Read 2 (in a paired-end context)
# -m: Discard reads shorter than MIN_READ_LENGTH after trimming
# -o: Output file for Read 1 (untrimmed, but kept in sync with Read 2)
# -p: Output file for Read 2 (trimmed)
cutadapt -A "${ADAPTER_SEQUENCE_R2}" \
         -m "${MIN_READ_LENGTH}" \
         -o "${OUTPUT_R1}" \
         -p "${OUTPUT_R2}" \
         "${INPUT_R1}" \
         "${INPUT_R2}"

steps.description: Command: cutadapt -f fastq --match-read-wildcards --times 1 -e 0.1 -O 5 --quality-cutoff 6 -m 18 -A AACTTGTAGATCGGA -A AGGACCAAGATCGGA -A ACTTGTAGATCGGAA -A GGACCAAGATCGGAA -A CTTGTAGATCGGAAG -A GACCAAGATCGGAAG -A TTGTAGATCGGAAGA -A ACCAAGATCGGAAGA -A TGTAGATCGGAAGAG -A CCAAGATCGGAAGAG -A GTAGATCGGAAGAGC -A CAAGATCGGAAGAGC -A TAGATCGGAAGAGCG -A AAGATCGGAAGAGCG -A AGATCGGAAGAGCGT -A GATCGGAAGAGCGTC -A ATCGGAAGAGCGTCG -A TCGGAAGAGCGTCGT -A CGGAAGAGCGTCGTG -A GGAAGAGCGTCGTGT -o /full/path/to/files/file_R1.C01.fastq.gz.adapterTrim.round2.fastq.gz -p /full/path/to/files/file_R2.C01.fastq.gz.adapterTrim.round2.fastq.gz /full/path/to/files/file_R1.C01.fastq.gz.adapterTrim.fastq.gz /full/path/to/files/file_R2.C01.fastq.gz.adapterTrim.fastq.gz > /full/path/to/files/file_R1.C01.fastq.gz.adapterTrim.round2.metrics
steps.code_example:
# Install cutadapt using conda (uncomment to run)
# conda install -c bioconda cutadapt

cutadapt -f fastq --match-read-wildcards --times 1 -e 0.1 -O 5 --quality-cutoff 6 -m 18 -A AACTTGTAGATCGGA -A AGGACCAAGATCGGA -A ACTTGTAGATCGGAA -A GGACCAAGATCGGAA -A CTTGTAGATCGGAAG -A GACCAAGATCGGAAG -A TTGTAGATCGGAAGA -A ACCAAGATCGGAAGA -A TGTAGATCGGAAGAG -A CCAAGATCGGAAGAG -A GTAGATCGGAAGAGC -A CAAGATCGGAAGAGC -A TAGATCGGAAGAGCG -A AAGATCGGAAGAGCG -A AGATCGGAAGAGCGT -A GATCGGAAGAGCGTC -A ATCGGAAGAGCGTCG -A TCGGAAGAGCGTCGT -A CGGAAGAGCGTCGTG -A GGAAGAGCGTCGTGT -o /full/path/to/files/file_R1.C01.fastq.gz.adapterTrim.round2.fastq.gz -p /full/path/to/files/file_R2.C01.fastq.gz.adapterTrim.round2.fastq.gz /full/path/to/files/file_R1.C01.fastq.gz.adapterTrim.fastq.gz /full/path/to/files/file_R2.C01.fastq.gz.adapterTrim.fastq.gz > /full/path/to/files/file_R1.C01.fastq.gz.adapterTrim.round2.metrics

steps.description: Takes output from cutadapt round 2.
steps.code_example:
# Install cutadapt (if not already installed)
# conda install -c bioconda cutadapt

# Define input and output file paths
# Assuming paired-end output from cutadapt round 2
INPUT_R1="output_from_cutadapt_round2_R1.fastq.gz"
INPUT_R2="output_from_cutadapt_round2_R2.fastq.gz"

# Define output file paths for this round
OUTPUT_R1="trimmed_round3_R1.fastq.gz"
OUTPUT_R2="trimmed_round3_R2.fastq.gz"

# Define adapter sequences for this round (replace with actual adapters if known)
# Example: Illumina universal adapter, Nextera adapter, or custom sequences
ADAPTER_R1="AGATCGGAAGAGCACACGTCTGAACTCCAGTCA"
ADAPTER_R2="AGATCGGAAGAGCGTCGTGTAGGGAAAGAGTGT"

# Execute cutadapt
# This command performs adapter trimming, quality trimming, and length filtering.
# Adjust -a, -A for specific adapters, -q for quality, --minimum-length for read length.
cutadapt \
  -a "${ADAPTER_R1}" \
  -A "${ADAPTER_R2}" \
  -q 20,20 \
  --minimum-length 50 \
  -o "${OUTPUT_R1}" \
  -p "${OUTPUT_R2}" \
  "${INPUT_R1}" \
  "${INPUT_R2}"

steps.description: Maps to human specific version of RepBase used to remove repetitive elements, helps control for spurious artifacts from rRNA (& other) repetitive reads.
steps.code_example:
# Installation:
# conda install -c bioconda bbtools

# Prepare your reference file for human repetitive elements and rRNA.
# This typically involves:
# 1. Downloading human-specific repetitive element sequences (e.g., from RepBase, often requires license).
# 2. Downloading human rRNA sequences (e.g., from NCBI, Ensembl, or dedicated rRNA databases).
# 3. Concatenating these FASTA files into a single reference file, e.g., 'human_repeats_rRNA.fasta'.
#    Example: cat human_repbase.fasta human_rRNA.fasta > human_repeats_rRNA.fasta
# Ensure the reference file is properly formatted FASTA.

bbduk.sh \
    in=raw_reads.fastq.gz \
    out=filtered_reads.fastq.gz \
    ref=human_repeats_rRNA.fasta \
    k=31 \
    hdist=1 \
    stats=bbduk_filtering_stats.txt \
    threads=8 \
    -Xmx4g

steps.description: Command: STAR --runMode alignReads --runThreadN 16 --genomeDir /path/to/RepBase_human_database_file --genomeLoad LoadAndRemove --readFilesIn /full/path/to/files/file_R1.C01.fastq.gz.adapterTrim.round2.fastq.gz /full/path/to/files/file_R2.C01.fastq.gz.adapterTrim.round2.fastq.gz --outSAMunmapped Within --outFilterMultimapNmax 30 --outFilterMultimapScoreRange 1 --outFileNamePrefix /full/path/to/files/file_R1.C01.fastq.gz.adapterTrim.round2.rep.bam --outSAMattributes All --readFilesCommand zcat --outStd BAM_Unsorted --outSAMtype BAM Unsorted --outFilterType BySJout --outReadsUnmapped Fastx --outFilterScoreMin 10 --outSAMattrRGline ID:foo --alignEndsType EndToEnd > /full/path/to/files/file_R1.C01.fastq.gz.adapterTrim.round2.rep.bam
steps.code_example:
# Install STAR using conda:
# conda install -c bioconda star

STAR --runMode alignReads --runThreadN 16 --genomeDir /path/to/RepBase_human_database_file --genomeLoad LoadAndRemove --readFilesIn /full/path/to/files/file_R1.C01.fastq.gz.adapterTrim.round2.fastq.gz /full/path/to/files/file_R2.C01.fastq.gz.adapterTrim.round2.fastq.gz --outSAMunmapped Within --outFilterMultimapNmax 30 --outFilterMultimapScoreRange 1 --outFileNamePrefix /full/path/to/files/file_R1.C01.fastq.gz.adapterTrim.round2.rep.bam --outSAMattributes All --readFilesCommand zcat --outStd BAM_Unsorted --outSAMtype BAM Unsorted --outFilterType BySJout --outReadsUnmapped Fastx --outFilterScoreMin 10 --outSAMattrRGline ID:foo --alignEndsType EndToEnd > /full/path/to/files/file_R1.C01.fastq.gz.adapterTrim.round2.rep.bam

steps.description: Takes output from STAR rmRep.
steps.code_example:
# Installation (uncomment to install via conda)
# conda install -c bioconda star

# The description "Takes output from STAR rmRep" is ambiguous as "rmRep" is not a standard STAR subcommand.
# Assuming the current step involves a typical STAR alignment, here is an example command.
# This command performs a basic two-pass alignment for RNA-seq reads.
# Replace placeholders like /path/to/genome_index, /path/to/read1.fastq.gz, etc.

STAR --runThreadN 8 \
     --genomeDir /path/to/genome_index \
     --readFilesIn /path/to/read1.fastq.gz /path/to/read2.fastq.gz \
     --outFileNamePrefix /path/to/output_dir/sample_ \
     --outSAMtype BAM SortedByCoordinate \
     --outBAMcompression 6 \
     --quantMode GeneCounts \
     --outFilterType BySJout \
     --outFilterMultimapNmax 20 \
     --outFilterMismatchNmax 999 \
     --outFilterMismatchNoverLmax 0.1 \
     --alignIntronMin 20 \
     --alignIntronMax 1000000 \
     --alignMatesGapMax 1000000 \
     --alignSJoverhangMin 8 \
     --alignSJDBoverhangMin 1 \
     --sjdbScore 1 \
     --readFilesCommand zcat

steps.description: Maps unique reads to the human genome.
steps.code_example:
# Installation (uncomment to run):
# conda install -c bioconda bwa

# Example: Index the reference genome (run once for a given reference)
# bwa index /path/to/human_genome.fa

# Example: Map paired-end reads to the human genome
# -t 8 specifies 8 threads for parallel processing
# /path/to/human_genome.fa is the indexed reference genome
# /path/to/reads_R1.fastq.gz and /path/to/reads_R2.fastq.gz are the input paired-end reads
# > aligned_reads.sam redirects the output to a SAM file
bwa mem -t 8 /path/to/human_genome.fa /path/to/reads_R1.fastq.gz /path/to/reads_R2.fastq.gz > aligned_reads.sam

steps.description: Command: STAR --runMode alignReads --runThreadN 16 --genomeDir /path/to/STAR_database_file --genomeLoad LoadAndRemove --readFilesIn /full/path/to/files/file_R1.C01.fastq.gz.adapterTrim.round2.rep.bamUnmapped.out.mate1 /full/path/to/files/file_R1.C01.fastq.gz.adapterTrim.round2.rep.bamUnmapped.out.mate2 --outSAMunmapped Within --outFilterMultimapNmax 1 --outFilterMultimapScoreRange 1 --outFileNamePrefix /full/path/to/files/file_R1.C01.fastq.gz.adapterTrim.round2.rmRep.bam --outSAMattributes All --outStd BAM_Unsorted --outSAMtype BAM Unsorted --outFilterType BySJout --outReadsUnmapped Fastx --outFilterScoreMin 10 --outSAMattrRGline ID:foo --alignEndsType EndToEnd > /full/path/to/files/file_R1.C01.fastq.gz.adapterTrim.round2.rmRep.bam
steps.tool_version: Not directly inferable from command, assuming a recent stable version (e.g., 2.7.x)
steps.code_example:
# Install STAR (example using conda):
# conda install -c bioconda star

STAR --runMode alignReads --runThreadN 16 --genomeDir /path/to/STAR_database_file --genomeLoad LoadAndRemove --readFilesIn /full/path/to/files/file_R1.C01.fastq.gz.adapterTrim.round2.rep.bamUnmapped.out.mate1 /full/path/to/files/file_R1.C01.fastq.gz.adapterTrim.round2.rep.bamUnmapped.out.mate2 --outSAMunmapped Within --outFilterMultimapNmax 1 --outFilterMultimapScoreRange 1 --outFileNamePrefix /full/path/to/files/file_R1.C01.fastq.gz.adapterTrim.round2.rmRep.bam --outSAMattributes All --outStd BAM_Unsorted --outSAMtype BAM Unsorted --outFilterType BySJout --outReadsUnmapped Fastx --outFilterScoreMin 10 --outSAMattrRGline ID:foo --alignEndsType EndToEnd > /full/path/to/files/file_R1.C01.fastq.gz.adapterTrim.round2.rmRep.bam

steps.description: takes output from STAR genome mapping.
steps.code_example:
# Installation (commented out)
# conda install -c bioconda star

# Execution command for STAR genome mapping
# This command generates the aligned reads (e.g., BAM files) and other outputs
# that would then be "taken" by subsequent steps in a bioinformatics pipeline.
STAR --runThreadN 8 \
     --genomeDir /path/to/STAR_genome_index \
     --readFilesIn /path/to/reads_R1.fastq.gz /path/to/reads_R2.fastq.gz \
     --outFileNamePrefix my_sample_aligned_ \
     --outSAMtype BAM SortedByCoordinate \
     --outSAMunmapped Within \
     --outSAMattributes Standard \
     --quantMode GeneCounts

steps.description: Custom random-mer-aware script for PCR duplicate removal.
steps.code_example:
# Install UMI-tools using conda
# conda install -c bioconda umi-tools

# Example usage of UMI-tools dedup for PCR duplicate removal.
# This command assumes that Unique Molecular Identifiers (UMIs) have already been extracted
# and added to the BAM file, typically as a tag (e.g., 'XC' tag by umi_tools extract).
# Replace 'input.bam' with your input BAM file and 'output.dedup.bam' with your desired output file name.
# The --log parameter is optional but recommended for tracking the deduplication process.
umi_tools dedup \
  -I input.bam \
  -S output.dedup.bam \
  --log=dedup.log

steps.description: Command: barcode_collapse_pe.py --bam /full/path/to/files/file_R1.C01.fastq.gz.adapterTrim.round2.rmRep.bam --out_file /full/path/to/files/file_R1.C01.fastq.gz.adapterTrim.round2.rmRep.rmDup.bam --metrics_file /full/path/to/files/file_R1.C01.fastq.gz.adapterTrim.round2.rmRep.rmDup.metrics
steps.code_example:
# Installation for umi-tools, which provides UMI collapsing functionality and may include or inspire scripts like barcode_collapse_pe.py
# conda install -c bioconda umi-tools
# pip install umi-tools

barcode_collapse_pe.py --bam /full/path/to/files/file_R1.C01.fastq.gz.adapterTrim.round2.rmRep.bam --out_file /full/path/to/files/file_R1.C01.fastq.gz.adapterTrim.round2.rmRep.rmDup.bam --metrics_file /full/path/to/files/file_R1.C01.fastq.gz.adapterTrim.round2.rmRep.rmDup.metrics

steps.description: Takes output from barcode collapse PE.
steps.code_example:
# Install umi_tools using conda
# conda install -c bioconda umi-tools

# Execute umi_tools dedup to collapse reads based on UMIs and genomic position for paired-end data.
# This command assumes that UMIs have already been extracted and appended to the read names
# (e.g., using umi_tools extract) in the input BAM file.
# Replace 'input_aligned_with_umis.bam' with your actual input BAM file
# and 'output_deduplicated.bam' with your desired output file name.
# The --paired-end flag is essential for paired-end data.
# The --method unique is a common deduplication strategy.

umi_tools dedup \
    --in input_aligned_with_umis.bam \
    --out output_deduplicated.bam \
    --paired-end \
    --method unique \
    --log dedup.log

steps.description: Sorts resulting bam file for use downstream.
steps.code_example:
# Install samtools (if not already installed)
# conda install -c bioconda samtools=1.19

# Sort the BAM file by coordinate
samtools sort -o sorted_output.bam input.bam

steps.description: Command: java -Xmx2048m -XX:+UseParallelOldGC -XX:ParallelGCThreads=4 -XX:GCTimeLimit=50 -XX:GCHeapFreeLimit=10 -Djava.io.tmpdir=/full/path/to/files/.queue/tmp -cp /path/to/gatk/dist/Queue.jar net.sf.picard.sam.SortSam INPUT=/full/path/to/files/file_R1.C01.fastq.gz.adapterTrim.round2.rmRep.rmDup.bam TMP_DIR=/full/path/to/files/.queue/tmp OUTPUT=/full/path/to/files/file_R1.C01.fastq.gz.adapterTrim.round2.rmRep.rmDup.sorted.bam VALIDATION_STRINGENCY=SILENT SO=coordinate CREATE_INDEX=true
steps.code_example:
# Installation (using conda, for a modern Picard version. Note: The execution command uses a specific 'Queue.jar' which likely contains an older, embedded Picard version or requires a specific GATK 3.x setup. For older versions, direct download from Broad Institute archives might be necessary.)
# conda install -c bioconda picard

# Define variables for clarity
GATK_QUEUE_JAR="/path/to/gatk/dist/Queue.jar" # Path to the specific Queue.jar used in the original command
INPUT_BAM="/full/path/to/files/file_R1.C01.fastq.gz.adapterTrim.round2.rmRep.rmDup.bam"
OUTPUT_BAM="/full/path/to/files/file_R1.C01.fastq.gz.adapterTrim.round2.rmRep.rmDup.sorted.bam"
TMP_DIR="/full/path/to/files/.queue/tmp"

# Command execution
java -Xmx2048m -XX:+UseParallelOldGC -XX:ParallelGCThreads=4 -XX:GCTimeLimit=50 -XX:GCHeapFreeLimit=10 \
     -Djava.io.tmpdir="${TMP_DIR}" \
     -cp "${GATK_QUEUE_JAR}" \
     net.sf.picard.sam.SortSam \
     INPUT="${INPUT_BAM}" \
     TMP_DIR="${TMP_DIR}" \
     OUTPUT="${OUTPUT_BAM}" \
     VALIDATION_STRINGENCY=SILENT \
     SO=coordinate \
     CREATE_INDEX=true

steps.description: Takes output from sortSam, makes bam index for use downstream.
steps.code_example:
# Install samtools if not already available
# conda install -c bioconda samtools

# Example usage:
# Assuming 'sorted.bam' is the output from sortSam
samtools index sorted.bam

steps.description: Command: samtools index /full/path/to/files/file_R1.C01.fastq.gz.adapterTrim.round2.rmRep.rmDup.sorted.bam /full/path/to/files/file_R1.C01.fastq.gz.adapterTrim.round2.rmRep.rmDup.sorted.bam.bai
steps.code_example:
# Install samtools (e.g., using conda)
# conda install -c bioconda samtools

# Define input and output file paths
INPUT_BAM="/full/path/to/files/file_R1.C01.fastq.gz.adapterTrim.round2.rmRep.rmDup.sorted.bam"
OUTPUT_BAI="/full/path/to/files/file_R1.C01.fastq.gz.adapterTrim.round2.rmRep.rmDup.sorted.bam.bai"

# Execute the samtools index command
samtools index "${INPUT_BAM}" "${OUTPUT_BAI}"

steps.description: Takes inputs from multiple final bam files.
steps.code_example:
# Installation instructions (commented out)
# For example, using Conda:
# conda install -c bioconda samtools

# Execution command
# This command merges multiple input BAM files into a single output BAM file.
# Replace 'output.bam' with your desired output file name.
# Replace 'input1.bam', 'input2.bam', 'input3.bam' with your actual input BAM files.
# The '-@' flag specifies the number of threads to use for faster processing.
samtools merge -@ 4 output.bam input1.bam input2.bam input3.bam

steps.description: Merges the two technical replicates for further downstream analysis.
steps.code_example:
# Install samtools (if not already installed)
# conda install -c bioconda samtools
# apt-get update && apt-get install samtools

# Merge two technical replicates (e.g., BAM files) into a single output file.
# Replace 'replicate1.bam' and 'replicate2.bam' with your actual input files.
# Replace 'merged_replicates.bam' with your desired output file name.
samtools merge merged_replicates.bam replicate1.bam replicate2.bam

steps.description: Command: samtools merge /full/path/to/files/CombinedID.merged.bam /full/path/to/files/file_R1.C01.fastq.gz.adapterTrim.round2.rmRep.rmDup.sorted.bam /full/path/to/files/file_R1.D08.fastq.gz.adapterTrim.round2.rmRep.rmDup.sorted.bam
steps.code_example:
# Install samtools if not already installed
# conda install -c bioconda samtools

samtools merge /full/path/to/files/CombinedID.merged.bam /full/path/to/files/file_R1.C01.fastq.gz.adapterTrim.round2.rmRep.rmDup.sorted.bam /full/path/to/files/file_R1.D08.fastq.gz.adapterTrim.round2.rmRep.rmDup.sorted.bam

steps.description: Takes output from sortSam, makes bam index for use downstream.
steps.code_example:
# Installation (uncomment to install if not already available):
# conda install -c bioconda samtools=1.19

# Execution:
# Assuming 'sorted.bam' is the output from sortSam
samtools index sorted.bam

steps.description: Command: samtools index /full/path/to/files/CombinedID.merged.bam /full/path/to/files/CombinedID.merged.bam.bai
steps.code_example:
# Install samtools (if not already installed, uncomment one of the following lines):
# conda install -c bioconda samtools
# apt-get install samtools

samtools index /full/path/to/files/CombinedID.merged.bam /full/path/to/files/CombinedID.merged.bam.bai

steps.description: Takes output from sortSam.
steps.code_example:
# conda install -c bioconda picard
# Or download the jar: wget https://github.com/broadinstitute/picard/releases/download/2.27.4/picard.jar
# And run with java: java -jar picard.jar MarkDuplicates ...

# Assuming picard is in PATH (e.g., via conda environment)
picard MarkDuplicates \\
    I=input.sorted.bam \\
    O=output.dedup.bam \\
    M=dedup_metrics.txt \\
    ASSUME_SORTED=true \\
    VALIDATION_STRINGENCY=SILENT

steps.description: Only outputs the second read in each pair for use with single stranded peak caller.
steps.code_example:
# Install samtools using conda
# conda install -c bioconda samtools

# Execute: Filter a paired-end BAM file to keep only the second read in each pair.
# Input: input.bam (a paired-end BAM file)
# Output: second_reads.bam (a BAM file containing only the second reads)
samtools view -b -f 128 input.bam > second_reads.bam

steps.description: This is the final bam file to perform analysis on.
steps.code_example:
# Install samtools if not already present
# conda install -c bioconda samtools

# Perform detailed statistics on the final BAM file.
# This command generates a comprehensive report about the alignment, read counts, mapping quality, etc.
# Replace 'final.bam' with the actual path to your final BAM file.
# The output will be redirected to 'final.stats.txt'.
samtools stats final.bam > final.stats.txt

steps.description: Command: samtools view -hb -f 128 /full/path/to/files/CombinedID.merged.bam > /full/path/to/files/CombinedID.merged.r2.bam
steps.code_example:
# Install samtools (e.g., using conda)
# conda install -c bioconda samtools=1.19

# Execute the samtools command
samtools view -hb -f 128 /full/path/to/files/CombinedID.merged.bam > /full/path/to/files/CombinedID.merged.r2.bam

steps.description: Takes results from samtools view.
steps.code_example:
# Install samtools using conda (uncomment to run)
# conda install -c bioconda samtools

# Example: Extract mapped reads from a BAM file
# This command takes an input BAM file and outputs a new BAM file
# containing only the mapped reads (flag -F 4 excludes unmapped reads).
# Replace 'input.bam' with your actual input file and 'mapped_reads.bam' with your desired output file.
samtools view -b -F 4 input.bam > mapped_reads.bam

steps.description: Calls peaks on those files.
steps.code_example:
# Install MACS2 using Bioconda
# conda install -c bioconda macs2

# Example usage of MACS2 to call peaks on ChIP-seq data.
# This command assumes 'treatment.bam' (ChIP-seq reads) and
# 'control.bam' (input/control reads) are available.
# Adjust --format, --gsize, --qvalue, and --outdir as needed.

# -t: Treatment file (ChIP-seq reads)
# -c: Control file (Input/control reads)
# -f: Format of input files (e.g., BAM for single-end, BAMPE for paired-end, BED)
# -g: Genome size (e.g., hs for human, mm for mouse, or a specific number)
# -n: Experiment name, used as prefix for output files
# --outdir: Directory to save output files
# -q: Q-value cutoff for peak detection (FDR, default 0.05)
# --keep-dup: How to handle duplicate reads (e.g., 'all', 'auto', '1')

macs2 callpeak \
    -t treatment.bam \
    -c control.bam \
    -f BAM \
    -g hs \
    -n my_experiment \
    --outdir peaks_output \
    -q 0.05 \
    --keep-dup all

steps.description: Command: clipper -b /full/path/to/files/CombinedID.merged.r2.bam -s hg19 -o /full/path/to/files/CombinedID.merged.r2.peaks.bed --bonferroni --superlocal --threshold-method binomial --save-pickle
steps.code_example:
# Install CLIPper using conda (recommended for bioinformatics)
# conda install -c bioconda clipper

# Execute CLIPper command
clipper -b /full/path/to/files/CombinedID.merged.r2.bam -s hg19 -o /full/path/to/files/CombinedID.merged.r2.peaks.bed --bonferroni --superlocal --threshold-method binomial --save-pickle

raw_text:
Library strategy: eCLIP-seq
Takes output from raw files.  Run to trim off both 5â and 3â adapters on both reads. Command: quality-cutoff 6  -m 18  -a NNNNNAGATCGGAAGAGCACACGTCTGAACTCCAGTCAC  -g CTTCCGATCTACAAGTT -g CTTCCGATCTTGGTCCT  -A AACTTGTAGATCGGA -A AGGACCAAGATCGGA -A ACTTGTAGATCGGAA -A GGACCAAGATCGGAA -A CTTGT  AGATCGGAAG -A GACCAAGATCGGAAG -A TTGTAGATCGGAAGA -A ACCAAGATCGGAAGA -A TGTAGATCGGAAGAG -A CCAAGATCGGAAGAG -A GTAGATCGGAAGAGC -A CAAGATCGGAAGAGC -A TAGATCGGAAGAGCG -A AAGATCGGAAGAGCG -A AGATCGGAAGAGCGT -A GATCGGAAGAGCGTC -A ATCGGAAGAGCGTCG -A TCGGAAGAGCGTCGT -A CGGAAGAGCGTCGTG -A GGAAGAGCGTCGTGT  -o /full/path/to/files/file_R1.C01.fastq.gz.adapterTrim.fastq.gz  -p /full/path/to/files/file_R2.C01.fastq.gz.adapterTrim.fastq.gz  /full/path/to/files/file_R1.C01.fastq.gz /full/path/to/files/file_R2.C01.fastq.gz > /full/path/to/files/file_R1.C01.fastq.gz.adapterTrim.metrics
Takes output from cutadapt round 1. Run to trim off the 3â adapters on read 2, to control for double ligation events. Command: cutadapt -f fastq --match-read-wildcards  --times 1  -e 0.1  -O 5  --quality-cutoff 6  -m 18  -A AACTTGTAGATCGGA -A AGGACCAAGATCGGA -A ACTTGTAGATCGGAA -A GGACCAAGATCGGAA -A CTTGTAGATCGGAAG -A GACCAAGATCGGAAG -A TTGTAGATCGGAAGA -A ACCAAGATCGGAAGA -A TGTAGATCGGAAGAG -A CCAAGATCGGAAGAG -A GTAGATCGGAAGAGC -A CAAGATCGGAAGAGC -A TAGATCGGAAGAGCG -A AAGATCGGAAGAGCG -A AGATCGGAAGAGCGT -A GATCGGAAGAGCGTC -A ATCGGAAGAGCGTCG -A TCGGAAGAGCGTCGT -A CGGAAGAGCGTCGTG -A GGAAGAGCGTCGTGT  -o /full/path/to/files/file_R1.C01.fastq.gz.adapterTrim.round2.fastq.gz  -p /full/path/to/files/file_R2.C01.fastq.gz.adapterTrim.round2.fastq.gz  /full/path/to/files/file_R1.C01.fastq.gz.adapterTrim.fastq.gz  /full/path/to/files/file_R2.C01.fastq.gz.adapterTrim.fastq.gz > /full/path/to/files/file_R1.C01.fastq.gz.adapterTrim.round2.metrics
Takes output from cutadapt round 2.  Maps to human specific version of RepBase used to remove repetitive elements, helps control for spurious artifacts from rRNA (& other) repetitive reads.  Command: STAR  --runMode alignReads  --runThreadN 16  --genomeDir /path/to/RepBase_human_database_file --genomeLoad LoadAndRemove  --readFilesIn /full/path/to/files/file_R1.C01.fastq.gz.adapterTrim.round2.fastq.gz /full/path/to/files/file_R2.C01.fastq.gz.adapterTrim.round2.fastq.gz --outSAMunmapped Within  --outFilterMultimapNmax 30  --outFilterMultimapScoreRange 1  --outFileNamePrefix /full/path/to/files/file_R1.C01.fastq.gz.adapterTrim.round2.rep.bam --outSAMattributes All  --readFilesCommand zcat  --outStd BAM_Unsorted  --outSAMtype BAM Unsorted  --outFilterType BySJout  --outReadsUnmapped Fastx  --outFilterScoreMin 10  --outSAMattrRGline ID:foo  --alignEndsType EndToEnd > /full/path/to/files/file_R1.C01.fastq.gz.adapterTrim.round2.rep.bam
Takes output from STAR rmRep.  Maps unique reads to the human genome.  Command: STAR  --runMode alignReads  --runThreadN 16  --genomeDir  /path/to/STAR_database_file --genomeLoad LoadAndRemove  --readFilesIn  /full/path/to/files/file_R1.C01.fastq.gz.adapterTrim.round2.rep.bamUnmapped.out.mate1  /full/path/to/files/file_R1.C01.fastq.gz.adapterTrim.round2.rep.bamUnmapped.out.mate2  --outSAMunmapped Within  --outFilterMultimapNmax 1  --outFilterMultimapScoreRange 1  --outFileNamePrefix /full/path/to/files/file_R1.C01.fastq.gz.adapterTrim.round2.rmRep.bam  --outSAMattributes All  --outStd BAM_Unsorted  --outSAMtype BAM Unsorted  --outFilterType BySJout  --outReadsUnmapped Fastx  --outFilterScoreMin 10  --outSAMattrRGline ID:foo  --alignEndsType EndToEnd > /full/path/to/files/file_R1.C01.fastq.gz.adapterTrim.round2.rmRep.bam
takes output from STAR genome mapping.  Custom random-mer-aware script for PCR duplicate removal. Command: barcode_collapse_pe.py  --bam /full/path/to/files/file_R1.C01.fastq.gz.adapterTrim.round2.rmRep.bam  --out_file /full/path/to/files/file_R1.C01.fastq.gz.adapterTrim.round2.rmRep.rmDup.bam  --metrics_file /full/path/to/files/file_R1.C01.fastq.gz.adapterTrim.round2.rmRep.rmDup.metrics
Takes output from barcode collapse PE.  Sorts resulting bam file for use downstream.  Command: java  -Xmx2048m  -XX:+UseParallelOldGC  -XX:ParallelGCThreads=4  -XX:GCTimeLimit=50  -XX:GCHeapFreeLimit=10  -Djava.io.tmpdir=/full/path/to/files/.queue/tmp  -cp /path/to/gatk/dist/Queue.jar  net.sf.picard.sam.SortSam  INPUT=/full/path/to/files/file_R1.C01.fastq.gz.adapterTrim.round2.rmRep.rmDup.bam  TMP_DIR=/full/path/to/files/.queue/tmp  OUTPUT=/full/path/to/files/file_R1.C01.fastq.gz.adapterTrim.round2.rmRep.rmDup.sorted.bam  VALIDATION_STRINGENCY=SILENT  SO=coordinate  CREATE_INDEX=true
Takes output from sortSam, makes bam index for use downstream.  Command: samtools index  /full/path/to/files/file_R1.C01.fastq.gz.adapterTrim.round2.rmRep.rmDup.sorted.bam  /full/path/to/files/file_R1.C01.fastq.gz.adapterTrim.round2.rmRep.rmDup.sorted.bam.bai
Takes inputs from multiple final bam files.  Merges the two technical replicates for further downstream analysis.  Command: samtools  merge  /full/path/to/files/CombinedID.merged.bam  /full/path/to/files/file_R1.C01.fastq.gz.adapterTrim.round2.rmRep.rmDup.sorted.bam /full/path/to/files/file_R1.D08.fastq.gz.adapterTrim.round2.rmRep.rmDup.sorted.bam
Takes output from sortSam, makes bam index for use downstream.  Command: samtools  index  /full/path/to/files/CombinedID.merged.bam  /full/path/to/files/CombinedID.merged.bam.bai
Takes output from sortSam.  Only outputs the second read in each pair for use with single stranded peak caller.  This is the final bam file to perform analysis on.  Command: samtools view -hb -f 128  /full/path/to/files/CombinedID.merged.bam  >  /full/path/to/files/CombinedID.merged.r2.bam
Takes results from samtools view.  Calls peaks on those files.  Command: clipper  -b /full/path/to/files/CombinedID.merged.r2.bam  -s hg19  -o /full/path/to/files/CombinedID.merged.r2.peaks.bed  --bonferroni  --superlocal  --threshold-method binomial  --save-pickle
Genome_build: hg19
Supplementary_files_format_and_content: bed format, contains clusters of predicted RBP binding; column 4 contains -log10(p-value) and column 5 contains log2(fold-enrichment) in eCLIP versus paired size-matched input
Supplementary_files_format_and_content: bw format
